title: Gastric Emptying Tests: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:

When your stomach empties too slowly (longer than normal), it may be a sign of delayed gastric emptying, called gastroparesis . It's often difficult to find the cause of gastroparesis, but diabetes Â is the most common underlying cause since it can damage some of the nerves in your stomach. Other causes of gastroparesis can include:
Hypothyroidism 
Injury to your esophagus (the muscular tube that carries food and liquids from your mouth to the stomach), stomach, or small intestine during surgery 
Certain autoimmune diseases 
Nervous system disorders such as Parkinson's disease Â and multiple sclerosis 
A viral infection Â of your stomach 
If food stays in your stomach for too long, it can harden into a solid mass called a bezoar. A bezoar may lead to a dangerous blockage that prevents food from passing into your small intestine.
Gastroparesis may also cause malnutrition Â (not getting enough nutrients from your food).
When your stomach empties too rapidly (faster than normal), it may be a sign of dumping syndrome . Dumping syndrome happens when your stomach empties food too quickly into your small intestine. It is usually associated with surgery on your stomach (such as weight loss surgery ) or your esophagus.
